daily_fact: Starting in 2026, New York City will add the new 465 area code to meet growing demand for phone numbers across the five boroughs.
topline_md: New York’s week is led by a volatile mix of stormy weather and major road closures, while politics and public policy intensify with early voting underway, new protest restrictions, and debates over affordability, health care, and education access.

items.title: Stormy Sunday night before dangerous summer heat surge
items.why_it_matters: Heavy rain, storms, and then intense heat could disrupt travel and raise health risks citywide.

items.title: Weekend parades and festivals bring NYC road closures
items.why_it_matters: Street closures can delay driving, buses, and deliveries across affected neighborhoods this weekend.

items.title: Early voting opens with 16,500-plus ballots cast statewide
items.why_it_matters: Strong first-day turnout signals active local elections that will shape city and state representation.

items.title: Hochul imposes new restrictions on protests in New York
items.why_it_matters: The changes could directly affect how New Yorkers demonstrate, gather, and interact with police.

items.title: Correction captain indicted in extortion and wage fraud case
items.why_it_matters: The indictment raises accountability concerns inside a city jail system funded by taxpayers.

items.title: Bill targets price disparities hurting independent New York grocers
items.why_it_matters: If enacted, the measure could affect neighborhood food prices and small-store survival.

items.title: Disabled New Yorkers challenge doctor-assisted death law
items.why_it_matters: The debate could influence state health policy and protections for vulnerable residents.

items.title: What to do if Essential Plan coverage ends July 1
items.why_it_matters: New Yorkers losing health coverage soon need immediate guidance to avoid gaps in care and higher costs.

items.title: Few NYC public schools have librarians, data shows
items.why_it_matters: Limited librarian access can hurt student literacy, research skills, and equitable school resources citywide.

items.title: Manhattan conversions could add nearly 3,000 housing units
items.why_it_matters: More apartments could modestly expand housing supply in a city facing persistent affordability pressures.

items.title: Bronx STEAM center planned for NYC public schools
items.why_it_matters: A new Bronx facility could expand hands-on science and technology opportunities for public school students.

items.title: Plans approved for 420-foot Downtown Brooklyn tower
items.why_it_matters: A major new tower can reshape housing, construction activity, and neighborhood development in Downtown Brooklyn.

items.title: No Kings concert planned in NYC Sunday
items.why_it_matters: The event could affect crowds, transit, and public activity depending on turnout and location.

items.title: 17th Polish Festival returns to Snug Harbor
items.why_it_matters: A large Staten Island festival brings community gatherings, local business traffic, and possible nearby congestion.
